title: Learn 80% of Claude Cowork in Under 20 Minutes
summary: Claude Cowork is Anthropic's desktop app that extends Claude from a chatbot into a full productivity system. It enables local file access, persistent memory, connectors, skills, projects, browser extension, and scheduled tasks for automating workflows like expense reports and inbox triage.
writeup: This video breaks down seven core capabilities of Claude Cowork, Anthropic's native desktop app, contrasting it directly with Claude Chat across file limits, context window size, output delivery, and prompting style. The central through-line is that Cowork's local file access unlocks persistent memory, multi-tool connector workflows, and outcome-first automation that Chat cannot replicate. Concrete walkthroughs cover expense report extraction from 100-plus receipts, meeting transcript cross-referencing across Google Drive and Notion, and writing-style extraction from Gmail history.
key_points: Claude Cowork removes Claude Chat's 20-file and 30-megabyte-per-file limits by accessing local files directly, and provides a larger context window that delays the compacting summarization cutoff.

Prompting Cowork requires outcome-first language (define the end result, constraints, and quality bar) rather than Chat's task-first, step-by-step instructions, because Cowork executes and delivers ready-to-use files rather than returning text a user must act on.

Persistent memory in Cowork is stored as actual files on disk (CLAUDE.md and memory.md) rather than online, so it holds an unlimited number of remembered preferences and decisions across sessions without the hard capacity cap that applies to Claude Chat.

Connectors extend Cowork's reach into external tools including Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Drive, and Notion, enabling cross-tool workflows such as pulling a Gemini-generated meeting transcript from Drive and comparing it against Notion notes to surface missed commitments.

Skills let users attach reusable prompt templates to a Cowork conversation so that a frequently used prompt (such as a clarity-and-conciseness rewrite) can be invoked in one click rather than retyped.
For tools not available as native connectors, Cowork supports custom MCP integrations, giving it extensibility beyond the built-in connector catalog.
